reasoning: Russia's potential membership in the 'Board of Peace' is contingent on a highly complex and unlikely condition: the US unfreezing Russian assets to cover the $1 billion membership fee. The Kremlin explicitly stated they would consult with 'strategic partners' and condition their participation on this asset transfer. In line with this friction, Putin and Lukashenko skipped the first Board of Peace meeting on February 19, 2026. On February 26, Moscow further criticized the initiative by questioning how it would coexist with the UN Security Council. There have been no March updates to indicate any diplomatic breakthrough, and reaching one by March 31 (in just two weeks) seems practically impossible given the stakes of unfreezing Russian sovereign assets. Thus, the probability of formal membership by the deadline is very low.
